chunks.text: Book Cory and Soda Hall Rooms - EECS at Berkeley Book Rooms in Cory and Soda Halls Rooms in Soda and Cory Halls are only available for EECS and other COE community events and cannot be reserved by members of the broader campus community. Review Room Booking Guidelines In This Page Large and medium-sized rooms in Cory & Soda Small and special purpose rooms in Soda Hall Alcoves, atrium, GSR rooms, etc. View or Subscribe to Room Calendars Directly on bCal For people who frequently book the same rooms. How to Book a Room Booking rooms in Cory and Soda Halls via a bCal room resource calendar is a three-step process: View room availability in the tables below to find a room that meets your needs. At the Google login screen, log in with your full @berkeley.edu email address, click Next, then supply your CalNet passphrase. Create an event on your own calendar: Log into bCal.berkeley.edu On your own calendar, begin to create an event as you normally would Click “Edit event” On the right side of the window, click the “Rooms, etc.” link under Add. Type in “Cory” or “Soda” to limit it to one building. Be sure that the “Show only available” box is checked! Click “Add” next to the room you want. Configure the rest of your event settings and click “Save”. Please note that all events must include a name and description. Include the following information in the description: Event description EECS Affiliation (i.e., course, organization,

the “Show only available” box is checked! Click “Add” next to the room you want. Configure the rest of your event settings and click “Save”. Please note that all events must include a name and description. Include the following information in the description: Event description EECS Affiliation (i.e., course, organization, etc.) Number of participants Speaker list and affiliation (if applicable) We recommend that you do not invite guests until after your event has been confirmed by the room scheduler . Receive meeting confirmation within 4 business days. THE BOOKING WILL NOT BE COMPLETE UNTIL YOU RECEIVE EMAIL FROM THE ROOM SCHEDULER APPROVING YOUR EVENT. Important Please submit requests at least four business days in advance to guarantee that reservations are approved on time. Reservations are only approved during business hours (M-F, 8 am-4:30 pm). Only the following subset of EECS-affiliated student groups may book rooms in Cory and Soda Halls. If your group is on the list, you may check availability by signing in with your @berkeley.edu email address and then your CalNet credentials Book a room by creating an event on your own calendar–do not try to book them on the “check availability” calendars. Follow the instructions in the How To column on the left. All events must include a name and description. Include the following information under the description box of your event: Event description EECS affiliation (i.e. course, organization, etc.) Number of participants Speaker list and affiliation (if applicable). Failure to include the above information in your

How To column on the left. All events must include a name and description. Include the following information under the description box of your event: Event description EECS affiliation (i.e. course, organization, etc.) Number of participants Speaker list and affiliation (if applicable). Failure to include the above information in your reservation will result in delays/cancellation. The room schedulers reserve the right to cancel or move any confirmed booking if the room is urgently needed for an important academic or department function. Please read the room restrictions and guidelines before booking rooms. Scroll down to see separate tables for alcoves and special purpose rooms in Soda Hall. Cory Hall Rooms Room Name/Number Cap. Notes (Restrictions/Layout) Availability Cory Courtyard 2nd floor 143 Layout: Open air, not covered Unfurnished (use of Tea1 tables is not permitted). Contact Cory building manager (cory-building@eecs) about furniture arrangements There are two entrances to the courtyard on the 2nd floor of Cory Hall: 1) through TI Lounge (room 246) on the north hallway (Hearst entrance) and 2) across from room 289 on the south hallway (near the passenger elevators). Check Availabilityof Cory Courtyard 540AB Cory (formerly the DOP Center Room) 46 Restrictions: This is a limited-use room. Read the room use policy to learn more. Available after 5 p.m. M-F and weekends Contact Cory hall front desk staff ( eecsoffice@eecs ) for exceptional approval to use the room You must have card key access to host a meeting in this room Layout: Amenities & photos for 540AB Cory

Notes (Restrictions/Layout) Availability 430-438 Soda Wozniak Lounge 100 Restrictions: No homework parties in this space between 11 am-2 pm, Mon-Fri. CoE departments, research centers, and non-EECS groups are also subject to a cleaning fee for events during the evening, weekend, or several hours during a weekday. You must have card key access to host a meeting in this room Layout: Room has a side kitchen and patio Amenities & photos for 430-438 Soda Check Availability of 430-438 Soda Wozniak Lounge Outside Terrace 100 Restrictions: The Outside Terrace Area may be freely used by EECS students during the day, but cleared by sunset. It may be closed without notice for Department-hosted meetings and events. Cannot be booked as a meeting space. 306 Soda HP Auditorium 98 Restrictions: No food or beverages For

Terrace 100 Restrictions: The Outside Terrace Area may be freely used by EECS students during the day, but cleared by sunset. It may be closed without notice for Department-hosted meetings and events. Cannot be booked as a meeting space. 306 Soda HP Auditorium 98 Restrictions: No food or beverages For office hours and homework parties, you can begin making reservations during the 2nd week of classes. For all other events, you have to wait until week 4 to book classrooms for reservations scheduled M- F before 8 pm. Evening and weekend reservations can be made according to the standard advance booking rules If your event starts before 9:00 am or after 4:00 pm on a weekday, or any time on the weekend, you must make an appointment to pick up a metal key at least 48 hours in advance of your event. Notes (Restrictions/Layout) Availability 734 Soda 4 Use limited to: Graduate student research meetings DSP/alternate exams Academic integrity discussions Check Availability of 734 Soda 732 Soda 3 Use limited to: Graduate student research meetings DSP/alternate exams Academic integrity discussions Check Availability of 732 Soda View or Subscribe to Room Calendars Directly on bCal When viewing calendars via the “Check Availability” links in the rooms tables, they will occasionally appear blank due to an intermittent problem with bCal. If that happens, use this method to view the room’s calendar directly from bCal: Open your personal bCal calendar. In the left column, click the arrow to the RIGHT of “Other calendars”. Select “Browse Interesting Calendars”. Select the “More” tab. Select “Resources for berkeley.edu” Scroll down to find the building you want and select it. You can either choose Preview to just get a quick look at the calendar, or Subscribe. If you subscribe, it will be added to your list of Other calendars and you’ll be able to look at it any time you want. View Open Faculty Positions
